text: The surgeon begins a laparoscopic cholecystectomy using a harmonic scalpel below the Rouviere's sulcus. The posterior leaf of the gallbladder's peritoneum is opened while maintaining the Teflon and silicon jaw of the harmonic towards the gallbladder to prevent rupture. Anterolateral traction is applied, with the silicon jaw positioned towards the liver for slight retraction. The harmonic tip is used to carefully open the peritoneum over the cystic pedicle without puncturing vessels, gradually stripping the peritoneum around the area.

The surgeon performs further skeletonization using a Maryland dissector to create a window, revealing the inferior surface of the liver. The cystic duct, identified as thin, is skeletonized, and the Calot's triangle is visualized. The cystic duct is ligated using Mishra's knot with a 90 cm number one Vicryl suture. The artery is sealed with the harmonic device. The gallbladder is separated from the liver using anteromedial and anterolateral traction, maintaining a good areolar plane to avoid bleeding and accidental rupture. A 30-degree telescope is used to enhance visibility as the gallbladder is progressively detached from the liver.

The surgeon uses a cloth forceps to crush and remove a 22mm stone. The telescope is transferred to the epigastric port, while graspers are positioned in the umbilical port. The right hypochondriac port is used for the harmonic device. The appendix is deeply retrocecal, with the base visible but the tip obscured. A Maryland dissector is used to separate epiploic tissue, progressing from the base towards the tip of the appendix.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to carefully dissect towards the tip of the gallbladder, ensuring the vibrating jaw does not contact the small bowel or cecum. The gallbladder is gradually lifted and separated, with the surgeon proceeding in an anti-grade manner due to the deep retrocecal position of the appendix. The tip of the appendix becomes slightly visible, requiring careful traction to avoid rupture.

The surgeon dissects the tip of the appendix, which is partially free but mostly attached to surrounding fat, using skeletonization to reach the base. A pre-tied loop of number one Vicryl is applied at the base, and the knot pusher is positioned over the cecum to ensure complete appendectomy. The appendix is then separated using harmonic energy, sealing and burning the mucosa. An endo bag is introduced through the 10mm umbilical port to remove the appendix. The port is closed using a cobbler's technique with a suture loop and Vicryl.
